subject_topic: GCSE Physics: Kinetic Energy (deviated to GCSE Maths: Factors)
key_learnings: The tutor critically misidentified the student's topic of struggle after the first turn, switching from 'Kinetic Energy' (GCSE Physics) to 'factors' (GCSE Mathematics). This fundamental error rendered the subsequent Socratic attempts largely ineffective for the student's original learning objective.
Despite the topic mismatch, the tutor consistently avoided providing direct answers, adhering to the principle of forcing productive struggle by not leaking the solution.

The student's Thinking Ratio was 24%, falling significantly below the benchmark of >40%. This indicates low active student participation and high frustration, which was likely exacerbated by the tutor's irrelevant line of questioning.
